acts.title: The Gala
acts.body:
In the wake of the Siege of Boralus, House Duskmere has spent months funding repairs to Tiragarde. Tonight, they celebrate the work's completion with a gala at their estate.
As Champions of Azeroth, you were invited guests.
Then the toasts stopped.
Lord Alaric Duskmere is found dead.

acts.title: The Captain Takes Charge
acts.body: Captain Mira Thorne of the Boralus City Watch arrives before the body's even cold, and takes one look at the gathered, panicking guests. 'Nobody leaves. Every single one of you is a suspect until I say otherwise. That includes our Champions of Azeroth.' She turns to you. 'Which is exactly why I need your help. I can't watch this whole gala at once. Move through the grounds, talk to people, find out who actually had reason to want Alaric Duskmere dead. Motive first. We'll worry about how it was done after.'

acts.title: Investigate the Estate
acts.intro: Walk the grounds and talk to everyone: guests, suspects, staff. Most people here have nothing to hide. A few do. Pick up whatever you find and bring it back to the Evidence Table, every piece matters, and Captain Thorne won't move things forward until it's all there.

acts.title: The Suspect Board
acts.intro: Captain Thorne has laid out what you've found on a table by the gate. 'Alright. Drag forward everyone you believe actually had a real motive. Leave off anyone whose story checks out. When you're confident, submit it to me, and know that I'll send you back to think again if you've got it wrong.'

acts.title: The Frame-Up
acts.body: 'Good work,' Thorne says, looking over your list, and sends you back to the Evidence Table to log the last of what you've found. You're barely through the door when Commodore Brannigan Voss 'discovers' the murder weapon planted right among your things. Thorne's face goes cold. 'I gave you a chance,' she says, and means it. 'Take them down to the cells.'

acts.title: The Cells
acts.body: [PLACEHOLDER: playable jail escape not built yet. Design is confirmed: a held-window mechanic, a plate or trigger stays live for a short countdown after being tripped, so a solo player can reach the second point in time without needing a true simultaneous press. Uses the dungeon tile pack. For now: you talk your way past a bored guard, find the one loose bar Old Silas Corwin told you about weeks ago and never explained why, and slip out into the dark.]

acts.title: The Guild Hall Basement
acts.body: You come up through a trapdoor into a cellar stacked with crates and old banners, the Guild Hall's basement. Corwin is waiting, like he knew exactly when you'd surface. 'Nobody in this town wants Duskmere's business made public either,' he says. 'That works in your favor. Get out there, ask around, quietly. I'll be here when you've got something.'

acts.title: Into Town
acts.body: [PLACEHOLDER: playable town map not built yet. Built from the tavern, blacksmith, chapel, ruined temple, mage tower, and training arena packs. Open design questions: how many clue-gathering stops, and whether it's barter based, trading items between players and NPCs, or simple talk-to-NPC. Depends on how many NPCs the finished town map ends up supporting. For now: you make the rounds quietly, and what you hear starts pointing at contradictions in what the five remaining suspects told Thorne earlier that night.]

acts.title: Means and Opportunity
acts.body: [PLACEHOLDER: the 5 Means and Opportunity puzzles are not built yet, content design is next. Back at the Guild Hall, you lay out what you gathered in town against what Ashby, Voss, Kestrel, Marrow, and Ashgate told Thorne, and the contradictions start to surface, one per suspect.]

acts.title: The Maid Returns
acts.body: [PLACEHOLDER, not built yet. The maid, cleared alongside Reyes back at the estate, turns up at the Guild Hall with something she'd been too frightened to hand over earlier: Lord Duskmere's own goblet, still faintly sticky at the rim. Whatever killed him, it went in through this.]

acts.title: The Herbalist's Hut
acts.body: [PLACEHOLDER: playable poison-ID puzzle not built yet. Design is confirmed: plant-matching deduction, leaf shape, smell, growing conditions, matched against a reference chart rather than a straight cipher, at a Herbalist's Hut out in the swamp. For now: the herbalist takes one look at the residue in the goblet and names the plant without hesitation.]

acts.title: Back to the Estate
acts.body: You make your way back to Duskmere Estate with everything: motive, contradiction, and now the poison itself. Thorne is waiting at the gate.

acts.title: The Commodore's Challenge
acts.body: Commodore Voss steps in before Thorne can even speak. 'If you've escaped from a City Watch cell,' he says, folding his arms, 'you had better have solved it.'

acts.title: Point the Finger
acts.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER: real accusation-vote mechanic not built yet, the party votes and submits who they believe did it, this typed-answer box is a stopgap using the same code path as the old Naming the Culprit act. Everyone agree on a name and type it below.]
acts.hint: Weigh the motive, the contradiction, and the poison together.

acts.title: Case Closed... Or Is It?
acts.body: The guards take Lady Isobel Ashgate away, and she goes without much of a fight, almost too composed for someone just caught out. But as they lead her past the garden, she rounds on Old Silas Corwin, still watching from the shadows. 'Ask him,' she spits, 'what he was really doing for Alaric all these years.' Corwin doesn't answer. Thorne tells you the case is closed and sends you home. Take your findings back to Discord to see if it really is.
